pairs.question: Who was George Ernest Cording and what was his role in cricket?
pairs.answer: George Ernest Cording was a Welsh cricketer born on 1 January 1878 in Tredegar, Monmouthshire. He was a right-handed batsman and a wicket-keeper in cricket.

pairs.question: When did George Ernest Cording make his first-class cricket debut for Glamorgan?
pairs.answer: George Ernest Cording made his first-class cricket debut for Glamorgan on 26 May 1921 against Sussex at Cardiff Arms Park, at the age of 43.

pairs.question: How many first-class matches did George Ernest Cording play for Glamorgan, and what were his career batting statistics?
pairs.answer: Cording played 19 first-class matches for Glamorgan. He scored a total of 498 runs at an average of 16.60, with a high score of 101, which was his only first-class century.

pairs.question: What notable achievement did George Ernest Cording accomplish in his cricket career?
pairs.answer: His most notable achievement was scoring his only first-class century, 101 runs, against Worcestershire at St Helens, Swansea, in 1921.

pairs.question: What were George Ernest Cording's contributions to cricket after retiring from playing?
pairs.answer: After retiring from cricket, Cording continued to promote cricket in South Wales. He helped raise war funds by organizing friendly matches during World War II.

pairs.question: What role did George Ernest Cording serve during World War II?
pairs.answer: During World War II, Cording served with the National Fire Service.

pairs.question: Where and when did George Ernest Cording pass away?
pairs.answer: George Ernest Cording died on 2 February 1946 at Llanrumney, Glamorgan.
